Algeria.text: Algeria scraped through the group stage in the most dramatic fashion: a 3-3 draw with Austria that swung violently in both directions, Mahrez equalising in stoppage time only for Kalajdzic to restore Austria's lead moments later — yet Algeria still advanced as one of the best third-placed sides. Switzerland await in the Round of 32 on Thu Jul 2 20:00 PT, a genuinely tight proposition; the Swiss are organised and difficult to break down, and Algeria's defensive frailties — seven goals conceded in the group — will be tested. Survive that, and Colombia or Ghana could follow, with Argentina a possible quarterfinal obstacle.

Argentina.text: Argentina closed the group stage in command, beating Jordan 3-1 in a performance that followed a familiar script: two goals before half-time settled the contest, and though Jordan pulled one back midway through the second half, Messi's late strike restored the margin and the sense of inevitability. Cape Verde await in the Round of 32 on Fri Jul 3 15:00 PT — a straightforward assignment on paper for the world champions, whose class and depth should prove decisive against African opposition of this level. Survive that, and Australia or Egypt loom in the last 16, before a potential quarterfinal collision with Colombia or Switzerland signals the point where Argentina's tournament truly begins.

Australia.text: Australia edged through Group D in second place after a goalless stalemate with Paraguay — a cautious, attritional finish that kept them alive but hardly advertised their ambitions. They face Egypt in the Round of 32 on Fri Jul 3 11:00 PT, a genuinely open contest between two sides of comparable standing, with neither able to claim a clear psychological edge heading into the last 32. Survive that, and Argentina could await in the Round of 16 on Tue Jul 7 09:00 PT — a fixture that would represent a steep step up in class and a genuine examination of how far this Socceroos side can go.

Austria.text: Austria scraped through Group J in the most dramatic fashion, surrendering a lead twice against Algeria before Kalajdzic's equaliser deep into stoppage time rescued a 3-3 draw that felt more like a reprieve than a result. The reward is a Round of 32 date with Spain on Thu Jul 2 12:00 PT — a formidable assignment by any measure, with Ralf Rangnick's side cast firmly as underdogs against one of the tournament's genuine heavyweights. Survive that, and Croatia or Portugal likely await in the Round of 16, raising the stakes further still.

Belgium.text: Belgium closed out the group stage in style, putting New Zealand to the sword 5-1 in a performance that was as commanding as the scoreline suggests — Trossard helping himself to a brace before Bruyne and Lukaku added gloss, the consolation arriving too late and too politely to matter. They face Senegal in the Round of 32 on Wed Jul 1 13:00 PT, a tie that favors the Belgians on paper but demands respect: the Senegalese are no pushover, and Belgium's group record of two draws alongside the win hints at a side still finding its sharpest gear. Should they progress, Portugal or Spain could await in the quarterfinal — the point at which this campaign would face its sternest examination.

Bosnia & Herzegovina.text: Bosnia & Herzegovina closed the group stage with purpose, beating Qatar 3-1 in a performance that was largely comfortable despite a brief scare when Al-Haydos pulled one back just before half-time — the Dragons had already struck twice inside the opening 35 minutes, and Mahmic's late third settled any nerves. They advance as one of the best third-placed teams, which earns them a Round of 32 date with host nation USA on Wed Jul 1 17:00 PT — a formidable assignment against a side carrying enormous home support, though Bosnia's attacking fluency makes this a tie rather than a foregone conclusion. Survive that, and Belgium or Senegal await in the last 16, with a potential quarterfinal collision with Portugal or Spain lurking deeper still.

Brazil.text: Brazil edged past Japan in a Round of 32 tie that was far less comfortable than their group-stage form had suggested — Sano's early goal gave the Samurai Blue a genuine foothold, and it took a Casemiro equaliser and a Martinelli winner deep into stoppage time to settle it. Norway await in the Round of 16 on Sun Jul 5 13:00 PT, a stern examination of Brazil's composure after that scare: the Scandinavians are a physical, well-organised side capable of disrupting even the most celebrated attacks, making this a real test rather than a formality. Should Brazil advance, England or Mexico could stand in the quarterfinal way, with France, Spain, or Portugal potentially looming as final-day opponents.

Canada.text: Canada edged past South Africa on the narrowest of margins, Eustáquio's stoppage-time winner sparing the hosts a nervy extra-time scenario and confirming that this side can grind out results when fluency deserts them. Morocco await in the Round of 16 on Sat Jul 4 10:00 PT, and few neutrals will call it straightforward — the Africans are a disciplined, tactically astute side, and Canada's slender victory does little to suggest they hold a clear edge. Survive that, and France could stand in the quarterfinal way, a test of an altogether different magnitude for a young Canadian side still finding its tournament rhythm.

Cape Verde.text: Cape Verde ground out a goalless draw against Saudi Arabia to close the group stage — three draws from three matches tells its own story of a side built on defensive solidity and collective discipline rather than creative spark. The reward is a Round of 32 appointment with Argentina on Fri Jul 3 15:00 PT, which is about as daunting a first knockout fixture as the draw could have produced: the world champions bring individual brilliance and tournament pedigree that make Cape Verde heavy underdogs, though a team that has conceded just twice in three matches will not be easily undone. Survive that, and Australia or Egypt await in the last sixteen — a far more navigable prospect that could open a genuinely improbable run deep into the tournament.

Colombia.text: Colombia closed out the group stage with a disciplined 0-0 stalemate against Portugal — a result that flattered neither side but confirmed Colombia as Group K winners, content to absorb pressure and deny a formidable opponent any daylight. They meet Ghana in the Round of 32 on Fri Jul 3 18:30 PT, a tie that places Colombia in the role of clear favourite: Ghana are no pushovers, but Colombia's defensive solidity and tournament pedigree make this a match they are expected to win without great alarm. Should they advance, Algeria or Switzerland await in the Round of 16, before a potential quarterfinal collision with Argentina that would announce Colombia's tournament in the loudest possible terms.

Croatia.text: Croatia edged past Ghana 2-1 in their final group match, Luka Sucic's first-half opener looking comfortable enough until Derek Luckassen leveled with seventeen minutes remaining — only for Nikola Vlasic to restore the lead seven minutes from time and spare Croatian nerves. They finish second in Group L and face Portugal in the Round of 32 on Thu Jul 2 16:00 PT, a formidable assignment against one of the tournament's weightier sides, though Croatia's tournament pedigree means they arrive as credible opponents rather than makeweights. Survive that, and either Spain or Austria awaits in the Round of 16, with the prospect of Belgium or the host-nation energy of the USA potentially looming in the quarterfinal beyond.

Curaçao.text: Curaçao's group stage ended with a 0-2 defeat to Ivory Coast, Nicolas Pépé doing the damage with goals either side of half-time — the first arriving inside seven minutes to set a tone the Curaçao defence never recovered from. They finished fourth in Group E with a single point and a goal difference of minus nine, yet still advanced to the Round of 32 as one of the tournament's best third-placed sides. There, their journey came to an abrupt end, the gulf in quality across the group stage ultimately a true reflection of where this Curaçao side stands on the world stage.

Czech Republic.text: Czech Republic's group stage ended with a thumping 3-0 defeat to Mexico, the goals arriving in a clinical second-half burst and a stoppage-time finish that underlined the gulf in class — a deflating exit for a side that managed just one point across three matches. Having finished fourth in Group A and crept through as one of the best third-placed teams, they were nonetheless unable to sustain that precarious foothold, and Mexico's composed, purposeful display exposed their limitations emphatically. The Czechs depart at the earliest opportunity, their tournament leaving little to build on.

DR Congo.text: DR Congo led England for the better part of an hour in the Round of 32, Cipenga's seventh-minute opener giving them every reason to believe before Harry Kane dismantled the dream with two clinical finishes in the final quarter-hour. It was a cruel, late unraveling — the Leopards had defended resolutely and threatened a genuine upset — only for England's captain to remind everyone why tournament football rewards patience and predatory quality. DR Congo exit at the last-32 stage, their campaign ultimately defined by the agony of a lead surrendered too late to recover.

Ecuador.text: Ecuador's exit arrived without much ceremony — a 0-2 defeat to Mexico in the Round of 32 in which they never truly threatened, conceding twice before half-time through Quiñones and a Jiménez strike deep in first-half stoppage time. The pattern was emphatic: Ecuador chasing the game from the half-hour mark, unable to find a response across 90 minutes. They had navigated the group stage only as one of the better third-placed sides, and against a Mexican outfit with genuine tournament ambition on home soil, that fragile foundation gave way entirely.

Egypt.text: Egypt edged through their final group fixture against Iran in uneasy fashion — Saber's fifth-minute opener was cancelled out by Rezaeian nine minutes later, and the Pharaohs had to settle for a point rather than the victory their early lead had promised. They advance from Group G in second place to face Australia in the Round of 32 on Fri Jul 3 11:00 PT, a tie Egypt will approach as moderate favourites but one in which the Socceroos, never easily dismissed at a tournament, will demand a disciplined, fully-committed performance. Survive that, and Argentina could await in the Round of 16 — a fixture that would represent a genuine examination of just how far this Egypt side can go.

England.text: England came through against DR Congo the hard way — trailing to Cipenga's early strike, they laboured for over an hour before Harry Kane dragged them level and then, with four minutes left, settled the tie with his second. It was late, nervy, and hardly a statement of intent. Mexico await in the Round of 16 on Sun Jul 5 17:00 PT, a genuinely open contest between two sides that consider themselves continental heavyweights — England enter as slight favourites on reputation, but a Mexican side on home soil will make this uncomfortable. Survive that, and Brazil or Norway could stand between England and the last four.

France.text: France dismantled Sweden 3-0 in the Round of 32 with a performance that brooked no argument — Mbappé scoring either side of half-time, Barcola adding a third, Sweden barely threatening throughout. Paraguay await in the Round of 16 on Sat Jul 4 14:00 PT, and Les Bleus will be heavy favourites against a side that has no business troubling one of the tournament's genuine contenders, barring something extraordinary. Should France advance through a potential quarterfinal meeting with Canada or Morocco, the semifinal bracket could yet produce Portugal or Spain — the kind of continental heavyweights capable of making this a proper examination.

Germany.text: Germany's Round of 32 exit is as brutal as the format can produce: Havertz's second-half equaliser cancelled out Enciso's opener, and when neither side could be separated across 90 minutes, Paraguay held their nerve in the shootout, converting four of four while Germany stumbled to three. A group-stage winner undone before the last sixteen, Joachim Löw's side — or whoever currently stewards this generation — departs a tournament they entered with genuine ambitions, beaten not by a superior team across the pitch but by the cold lottery of penalties.

Ghana.text: Ghana scraped through to the knockout rounds in third place after a bruising group finale against Croatia — a Luckassen equaliser briefly levelled things at 1-1, but Vlasic's 83rd-minute winner sealed a 2-1 defeat that underlined how slender the Black Stars' margins have been throughout. Colombia await in the Round of 32 on Fri Jul 3 18:30 PT, and the South Americans, fresh from their own group campaign, enter as clear favourites against a Ghana side that has looked vulnerable to organised pressing. Survive that, and Algeria or Switzerland could follow — with a potential quarterfinal meeting with Argentina lurking as the draw's most daunting prospect for the Black Stars.

Haiti.text: Haiti depart the 2026 World Cup without a point to their name, their group stage exit confirmed by a 4-2 defeat to Morocco that was comfortable for the Atlas Lions despite a spirited Haitian showing either side of half-time. Two goals inside the first 43 minutes gave Haiti brief cause for optimism, but Morocco reasserted themselves emphatically in the closing stages, scoring four times in total to send their opponents home. The Caribbeans finish bottom of Group C, undone by a gulf in quality that proved too wide to bridge across three matches.

Iran.text: Iran's group-stage finale against Egypt ended 1-1, a Rezaeian equaliser on 14 minutes cancelling out Saber's early opener — a pattern that defined their entire tournament: always in the contest, never quite through it. Three draws, no wins, and three points were enough to sneak through as one of the best third-placed sides, but the knockout round delivered the reckoning that the group stage had deferred. Iran exit the 2026 World Cup without a victory to their name, their campaign a study in stubborn resilience that ultimately fell short of the cutting edge a deep run demands.

Iraq.text: Iraq's group stage ended in humiliation against Senegal, a 5-0 defeat that was effectively settled inside the opening minutes and then ruthlessly extended across the second half, with Gueye alone scoring twice in a 12-minute spell. Finishing fourth in Group I with three losses, no points, and twelve goals conceded, Iraq's tournament was over at the earliest stage — a chastening introduction to a World Cup where the gulf in class between the continent's elite and the tournament's newcomers was laid painfully bare.

Ivory Coast.text: Ivory Coast gave Norway a genuine contest in the Round of 32, pulling level through Diallo on 74 minutes after falling behind to Nusa's first-half opener — only for Haaland to settle it with four minutes remaining, a late hammer blow that extinguished the comeback. The Elephants depart a tournament they had navigated with enough promise to suggest they belonged, yet ultimately could not withstand Norway's cutting edge when it mattered most.

Japan.text: Japan held their own against Brazil for the better part of an hour, Sano's 29th-minute opener giving them genuine belief before Casemiro levelled midway through the second half. It was Martinelli's stoppage-time winner that delivered the cruellest of exits — a Samurai Blue side that had navigated Group F without defeat ultimately undone in the final seconds of a Round of 32 tie they had come agonisingly close to taking further.

Jordan.text: Jordan's group stage ended with a 3-1 defeat to Argentina — a result that followed the script almost from the first whistle, with the South Americans establishing a two-goal cushion inside half an hour before Messi put the contest beyond doubt late on. Al-Tamari's consolation just after the hour offered a brief flicker, but Jordan were outclassed. Finishing fourth in Group J with three defeats and no points, they nevertheless advanced as one of the tournament's best third-placed sides before their World Cup came to a close.

Mexico.text: Mexico swept through Group A without conceding a goal and dispatched Ecuador with the same authority in the Round of 32, Quiñones and Jiménez both scoring before half-time to make it a thoroughly professional afternoon. England await in the Round of 16 on Sun Jul 5 17:00 PT — a genuine test of how far El Tri's ambitions can stretch, with Gareth Southgate's side carrying the weight of expectation and Mexico cast as dangerous but decided underdogs. Survive that, and Brazil or Norway could stand in the quarterfinal way, with the prospect of Argentina or Colombia looming beyond.

Morocco.text: Morocco's passage through the Round of 32 was as dramatic as knockout football gets: Gakpo put the Netherlands ahead with eighteen minutes remaining, only for Diop to level deep into stoppage time, and the Atlas Lions then edged a nervy shootout 3-2. That is the manner of side they are — resilient, hard to kill, dangerous when the stakes are highest. Canada await in the Round of 16 on Sat Jul 4 10:00 PT, a tie Morocco will approach as clear favourites against a host nation still finding its feet at this level. Survive that, and France or Paraguay could present a far sterner quarterfinal examination on the road to what might yet be a historic run.

Netherlands.text: Netherlands dominated their group with seven points and a healthy goal difference, only to suffer one of the tournament's crueller exits — Gakpo's 72nd-minute strike cancelled out by a Moroccan equaliser deep in stoppage time, before the Dutch lost the subsequent shootout 2-3. It was the worst kind of departure: a lead surrendered at the death, then the lottery of penalties delivering the final blow. A side that had looked like genuine contenders through the group stage goes home from the Round of 32, undone in the space of twenty minutes.

New Zealand.text: Belgium administered a thorough lesson in the final group game, with Trossard's brace and further goals from De Bruyne and Lukaku turning what might have been a contest into a rout — New Zealand's consolation arriving far too late, in the 84th minute, to suggest any real foothold. The All Whites finished fourth in Group G with a single point and a goals difference of minus ten, their tournament record a frank reflection of the gulf between them and the competition's established forces. They depart a World Cup that offered little comfort beyond the experience itself.

Norway.text: Norway edged past Ivory Coast in a nervy Round of 32 encounter, Nusa's first-half opener holding until Diallo levelled in the 74th minute before Haaland rescued it with a late winner four minutes from time. That dramatic finish sets up a Round of 16 meeting with Brazil on Sun Jul 5 13:00 PT — a genuine test of Norway's ambitions, with the Seleção heavy favourites and likely to demand far more than Ivory Coast offered. Survive that, and England or Mexico could await in the quarterfinal, with Argentina and Colombia among the heavyweights lurking further down the draw.

Panama.text: Panama's group stage exit was as comprehensive as the record suggests — three defeats, no goals scored, and a closing loss to England that was settled efficiently in a five-minute spell midway through the second half, Bellingham and Kane putting the game to bed without drama or late anxiety. They finish bottom of Group L with nothing to show from their three outings, and whatever hope they carried into the tournament evaporated long before the knockout rounds began. A familiar story for Central American football at this level: the gap between qualifying and competing proved too wide to bridge.

Paraguay.text: Paraguay squeezed past Germany in the Round of 32 in the most nerve-shredding fashion — level at 1-1 after ninety minutes, Enciso's first-half opener cancelled out by Havertz shortly after the break, before the Albirrojas held their nerve to win 4-3 on penalties. Now they face France in the Round of 16 on Sat Jul 4 14:00 PT, and few would back them to repeat the trick: this is a severe examination, with the French arriving as clear favourites and Paraguay's only realistic hope resting on the kind of stubborn, low-block resilience they showed against the Germans. Survive that, and Canada or Morocco await in the quarterfinal — with Spain, Portugal, or Brazil potentially looming beyond.

Portugal.text: Portugal closed out their group campaign with a goalless stalemate against Colombia — a measured, controlled affair that kept them unbeaten but confirmed second place behind the group winners. They carry genuine attacking menace into the knockout rounds, even if that final sharpness remained elusive on the night. Croatia await in the Round of 32 on Thu Jul 2 16:00 PT, a fixture Portugal enter as favorites but should not take lightly — Zlatko Dalić's side are seasoned knockout operators who have humbled bigger names before. Survive that, and Spain could loom in the Round of 16, with France a possible semifinal obstacle should Portugal's ambitions stretch as far as the last four.

Qatar.text: Qatar's group stage exit arrived with little ceremony — a 1-3 defeat to Bosnia & Herzegovina in which they trailed by two goals before half-time, Al-Haydos pulling one back on the stroke of the interval before Mahmic's 80th-minute strike killed any lingering hope of a comeback. The hosts finished bottom of Group B with a single point and a goal difference of minus eight, their tournament a sobering exercise in the gap between ambition and execution at this level.

Saudi Arabia.text: Saudi Arabia's group stage ended with a goalless draw against Cape Verde — a stalemate that was as flat as the scoreline suggests, neither side able to manufacture a breakthrough across ninety minutes. That result, combined with a goal difference of minus five across three matches, proved just enough to squeeze through as one of the best third-placed teams, but the Green Falcons were eliminated in the Round of 32, their tournament ending as quietly as it had begun.

Scotland.text: Brazil ended Scotland's tournament before it had truly begun, a 3-0 defeat that was every bit as one-sided as the scoreline suggests. Júnior struck twice in the first half — the second arriving deep in added time — before Cunha added a third shortly after the interval, leaving Scotland with nothing to build on. They depart having advanced from the group stage as one of the best third-placed sides, a modest distinction that flatters a campaign yielding just one win and four goals conceded. The 2026 World Cup ends for Scotland at the Round of 32.

Senegal.text: Senegal closed the group stage with a statement, dismantling Iraq 5-0 in a performance that brooked no argument — Diarra opening inside four minutes, Gueye helping himself to a brace in a ruthless second-half spell that rendered the scoreline entirely convincing. The reward is a Round of 32 date with Belgium on Wed Jul 1 13:00 PT, a genuinely difficult proposition: the Belgians carry the weight of European pedigree and Senegal, for all the swagger of that final group win, arrive as third-place qualifiers who dropped two matches along the way. Survive that, and Portugal or Spain could await in the quarterfinal — the kind of reckoning that will tell us exactly what this Lions squad is made of.

South Africa.text: South Africa's World Cup ended in the cruelest fashion, a 90th-minute goal from Eustáquio giving Canada a 1-0 win that the balance of the match may not have fully demanded. Bafana Bafana had navigated the group stage with enough grit to finish second in Group A, but a tournament played on home soil — or at least in their confederate hemisphere — ultimately closed in the dying seconds of a Round of 32 tie they very nearly survived.

South Korea.text: South Korea close their World Cup 2026 account with a flat 0-1 defeat to South Africa, Maseko's goal just past the hour mark enough to settle a match the Taeguk Warriors never truly threatened to turn. They had squeezed through the group phase as one of the best third-placed sides, but that slender lifeline ran out in the round of 32 — eliminated without a goal scored in their final two outings, their tournament ending as quietly as it had begun.

Spain.text: Spain closed out Group H with a composed, professional 1-0 win over Uruguay, Alex Baena's first-half strike doing exactly enough to confirm top spot without Luis de la Fuente's side ever needing to shift through the gears. Austria await in the Round of 32 on Thu Jul 2 12:00 PT — a manageable assignment for a side of Spain's pedigree, though the Austrians are no pushover and will test the backline's concentration. Survive that, and the draw opens into genuinely interesting territory: Croatia or Portugal could appear in the Round of 16, a heavyweight Iberian derby or a reunion with old European rivals, with France or Morocco potentially lurking as semifinal opponents should Spain's tournament run deep.

Sweden.text: Sweden's tournament ends with a 0-3 defeat to France in the Round of 32 — a verdict that was effectively delivered at half-time, Mbappé's first strike just before the break puncturing whatever hope the Swedes had carried into the tie, and a Barcola goal eight minutes into the second half rendering the contest settled long before his second. They had advanced from Group F as one of the better third-placed sides, a narrow path that always pointed toward exactly this kind of reckoning: a French side operating at a different altitude. The margin was honest.

Switzerland.text: Switzerland closed out the group stage with a controlled 2-1 win over Canada, taking command through two goals either side of the hour mark before absorbing a late consolation — comfortable enough to confirm top spot in Group B. They meet Algeria in the Round of 32 on Thu Jul 2 20:00 PT, a fixture that, on reputation and form, the Swiss enter as clear favourites, though Algeria are a resilient North African side capable of making life awkward. Survive that, and a potential quarterfinal collision with Argentina looms as the tie that could define their tournament.

Tunisia.text: Tunisia's group stage exit was written early: two Dutch goals inside seven minutes buried the Carthage Eagles before the match had found its shape, and though Mastouri pulled one back after the break, Hecke's strike shortly after the hour restored the three-goal cushion and sealed a 3-1 defeat. Tunisia finished bottom of Group F without a point, their record of three defeats and a goal difference of minus ten telling the story of a tournament in which they were consistently outclassed — a dispiriting campaign ended against one of the competition's heavyweights.

Turkey.text: Turkey's tournament ends in the cruelest of fashions — a 3-2 win over the United States that arrived too late to matter. They trailed inside three minutes, fought back to lead through Güler and Yilmaz, conceded again early in the second half, and only sealed it through Ayhan's injury-time strike. The drama was real, the effort undeniable, but a fourth-place group finish — even as one of the better third-placed sides — proved an insufficient foundation, and Turkey's World Cup 2026 is over at the group stage.

USA.text: USA finished top of Group D despite a bruising final group-stage defeat to Turkey — Arda Güler's early equaliser and a clinical Yilmaz strike put the Turks in command before an injury-time third buried any American hope of a comeback, the 3-2 scoreline a reminder that this side can be pierced. Bosnia & Herzegovina await in the Round of 32 on Wed Jul 1 17:00 PT, a winnable assignment for the hosts against a side without European heavyweights' pedigree, though the Turkish result shows the Americans cannot afford a slow start. Survive that, and Belgium or Senegal loom in the last 16, with Portugal or Spain potentially standing between the USA and a semifinal berth.

Uruguay.text: Uruguay's group stage ended with a narrow 0-1 defeat to Spain, Álex Baena's first-half strike the solitary difference in a contest that offered the Uruguayans little joy in the final third. Despite advancing as one of the best third-placed sides, their tournament came to an abrupt halt at the same juncture — two draws and a loss across Group H, with a negative goal difference, telling a story of a side that never quite found its footing at this World Cup.

Uzbekistan.text: Uzbekistan's group stage ended with a 1-3 defeat to DR Congo, a result that traced a familiar arc: an encouraging early lead through Shomurodov, then a second-half capitulation as Wissa and Mayele dismantled them after the hour mark, Wissa adding a third in stoppage time to put a harsh gloss on the scoreline. The Central Asians finished bottom of Group K without a point, conceding eleven goals across three matches, and no late reprieve arrived. Their debut on the expanded World Cup stage was brief and bruising, a tournament that exposed the gulf between ambition and readiness at the highest level.
